output:
**(B)**.

The structural pattern is the same in all three: **excellence at level N is treated as evidence of fitness for level N+1, but the two levels demand disjoint skill sets.** The promotion criterion (technical performance) is orthogonal to the requirement of the new role (management/coordination), so the transition reliably destroys value at both levels — you lose a strong contributor *and* gain a weak manager.

(A) is a surface-level takeaway, not a structural description. (C) and (D) are causal/prescriptive claims that miss the mechanism entirely.
